extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR won mountaintop sprint on Jebel Jais after UAE and Ineos trains neutralized repeated attacks, finishing alongside YATES and VLASOV
tactical_patterns: UAE Emirates systematically extinguished attacks rather than launching own moves, conserving Pogačar for final sprint.
Ineos used PLAPP to lift pace at 1km barriers creating sprint train, inadvertently helping UAE's sprint setup.
MICHIELS/VLASOV probing attack caught early on climb, suggesting UAE unwilling to concede even small time gaps.
HIRT/TERAMAE softening tactics for Intermarché despite having multiple GC cards showed aggressive team-first approach.

rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: negative: drifted badly on climb after strong third-place TT, suggesting poor recovery or TT effort too deep
rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: finished two seconds down on mountain stage, unexpectedly strong for pure TT specialist
rider_signals.PLAPP Luke: neutral: Australian champion worked at steepest section but effort ultimately served Pogačar not Yates
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: top-ten TT finish suggests exceptional form carrying into sprint stages beyond typical sprinter baseline
